title: HELIX docs backfill: repo
description: Repository-scope HELIX documentation backfill for grctool. Reconstruct canonical artifacts from current evidence, record confidence, and file follow-up work where guidance or execution is still needed.
acceptance:
1. Repo scope is inventoried with explicit coverage ownership or exclusions.
2. Durable backfill report is written under docs/helix/06-iterate/backfill-reports/.
3. High-confidence canonical HELIX artifacts are updated where evidence supports them.
4. Remaining ambiguity and follow-up work are captured in tracker issues or guidance gates.

title: Review CLI and configuration surface
description: Review main entrypoints, Cobra commands, configuration loading, logging bootstrap, and command UX surfaces.
acceptance: 1. main.go, cmd/, configs/, and config-related docs are surveyed.\n2. Current CLI/configuration behavior is summarized with evidence.

title: Review CI, release, and operational delivery surface
description: Review GitHub workflows, Make targets, scripts, release automation, and deployment/operational docs.
acceptance: 1. .github/workflows, scripts/, Makefile, and deployment docs are surveyed.\n2. Delivery constraints and release drift are summarized with evidence.

title: Review evidence, AI, and tool framework
description: Review evidence workflows, tool registry, provider contracts, document generation, and AI-assisted evidence surfaces.
acceptance: 1. internal/tools, internal/services, internal/evidence, internal/templates, internal/formatters, and related docs are surveyed.\n2. Evidence/tool architecture and current capability boundaries are summarized with evidence.

title: Review provider, auth, storage, and sync subsystems
description: Review authentication, provider registry, Tugboat client, storage, scheduler, and sync subsystems for runtime architecture and operational constraints.
acceptance: 1. internal/auth, internal/providers, internal/storage, internal/sync, internal/scheduler, and internal/tugboat are surveyed.\n2. Runtime architecture and operational constraints are summarized with evidence.

title: Consolidate repo backfill findings
description: Consolidate area review findings into a repo-scope backfill report, update high-confidence HELIX artifacts, and create follow-up issues.
acceptance:
1. Coverage ledger is complete.
2. Durable backfill report is written.
3. High-confidence HELIX artifacts are updated.
4. Follow-up issues are created for remaining gaps.

title: Review docs and product surface
description: Review non-HELIX docs plus canonical discover/frame artifacts for current product intent, feature inventory, and terminology drift.
acceptance: 1. docs/, README.md, and canonical discover/frame artifacts are surveyed.\n2. Product behavior and terminology drift are recorded with confidence.

title: Review tests and quality gates
description: Review unit, integration, functional, and e2e tests, VCR fixtures, benchmarks, mutation outputs, and testing docs.
acceptance: 1. test/, test-integration/, internal test surfaces, and testing docs are surveyed.\n2. Current 4-tier test strategy and quality gate drift are summarized with evidence.

title: Align CI and release workflows with Go 1.24 baseline
description: Update GitHub workflow Go versions, test assumptions, and related docs so CI/release automation matches the repository's Go 1.24 baseline declared in go.mod and AGENTS.md.
acceptance: 1. CI and release workflows use a Go version compatible with go.mod.\n2. Related HELIX/testing docs no longer conflict with the automation baseline.\n3. Validation commands are updated if workflow assumptions changed.

title: Normalize HELIX solution design IDs and traceability
description: The current HELIX design stack contains ambiguous solution-design naming, including multiple SD-001 variants. Normalize IDs and cross-phase references so design artifacts have a deterministic traceability scheme.
acceptance: 1. Solution design filenames and identifiers are unambiguous.\n2. Cross-references from frame, design, and build docs resolve cleanly.\n3. Any superseded IDs are documented rather than silently renamed.

title: Clarify roadmap language for system-of-record ambitions
description: Discover/frame artifacts describe GRCTool as evolving into a system of record with broader provider scope, while current implementation remains primarily Tugboat-centric. Clarify which statements are present-day commitments versus longer-term direction.
acceptance: 1. Product vision and PRD distinguish current behavior from roadmap intent.\n2. Feature and design docs use consistent terminology for current versus future scope.\n3. Any guidance-dependent claims are explicitly marked or resolved.

title: HELIX alignment review: repo
description: Repository-scope HELIX alignment review for grctool. Reconstruct intent from the canonical planning stack, audit implementation and tests against that authority, consolidate findings in a durable report, and create deterministic execution issues for uncovered gaps.
acceptance:
1. Repo review scope is decomposed into functional review areas with tracker issue ownership.
2. Canonical HELIX planning artifacts are reconciled against implementation and tests.
3. Durable alignment report is written under docs/helix/06-iterate/alignment-reviews/.
4. Every non-aligned gap is represented by deterministic execution issues or explicit deferral rationale.

title: Review planning intent and traceability
description: Review the product vision, PRD, feature specs, user stories, architecture, solution designs, test plans, and implementation plans for repo-scope consistency and top-down traceability.
acceptance:
1. Canonical planning artifacts are summarized without using source code to fill gaps.
2. Cross-layer contradictions, stale artifacts, and missing links are identified with evidence.
3. Findings are captured in the consolidated alignment report with an owning review issue reference.

title: Review provider framework and Tugboat sync runtime
description: Review the provider interfaces, registry, Tugboat provider, storage extensions, sync service, and sync orchestrator against FEAT-004 and related design authority.
acceptance:
1. FEAT-004 acceptance criteria are checked against implementation and tests.
2. Runtime surfaces and unresolved gaps are classified with planning and implementation evidence.
3. Results are recorded in the alignment report and gap register with this review issue as owner.

title: Review external sync adapters
description: Review the AccountableHQ and Google Drive sync providers, related converter code, and their alignment with FEAT-001, FEAT-002, SD-001, and SD-002.
acceptance:
1. FEAT-001 and FEAT-002 acceptance criteria are checked against implementation and tests.
2. Any partial implementations, unsupported entity types, or missing conflict flows are documented with evidence.
3. Findings are represented in the consolidated report and linked to deterministic execution issues where needed.

title: Review lifecycle and scheduling behavior
description: Review lifecycle state machines, scheduler implementation, schedule CLI wiring, and orchestration behavior against FEAT-003 and SD-003.
acceptance:
1. FEAT-003 acceptance criteria are checked against implementation and tests.
2. Any placeholder, dry-run-only, or partially wired lifecycle and scheduler behavior is documented with evidence.
3. Findings are reflected in the gap register with this review issue as owner.

title: Review evidence generation, submission, and tool workflows
description: Review evidence generation, evaluation, review, submission, and the evidence collection tool framework against FEAT-008, FEAT-009, and FEAT-010.
acceptance:
1. Evidence and tool feature acceptance criteria are checked against implementation and tests.
2. User-facing workflow coverage, quality gates, and submission behavior are classified with evidence.
3. Findings are consolidated into the report and execution issue set when gaps remain.

title: Review tests, CI, and operational quality gates
description: Review the test tiers, VCR strategy, functional/e2e coverage, Make targets, and CI/release automation for consistency with the HELIX test and build stack.
acceptance:
1. Test-plan and build-plan expectations are checked against repo tests and automation.
2. Any failing or stale quality-gate assumptions are documented with evidence.
3. Drift that requires follow-up work is captured in the report and execution issue set.

title: Review CLI, config, and auth surfaces
description: Review CLI framework (FEAT-005), browser auth (FEAT-006), config loading, and logging bootstrap against feature specs and acceptance criteria.
acceptance: 1. FEAT-005 and FEAT-006 acceptance criteria are checked against implementation and tests.\n2. Findings recorded in alignment report.
notes: Superseded by the consolidated planning/CLI/auth review scope used in AR-2026-04-01-repo.

title: Review Tugboat sync subsystem
description: Review Tugboat data sync (FEAT-007) including sync service, Tugboat client, storage, and VCR coverage against feature spec acceptance criteria.
acceptance: 1. FEAT-007 acceptance criteria are checked against implementation and tests.\n2. Findings recorded in alignment report.
notes: Superseded by the consolidated provider framework and Tugboat runtime review scope used in AR-2026-04-01-repo.

title: Review evidence tools and AI generation
description: Review evidence collection tools (FEAT-008), AI evidence generation (FEAT-009), and evidence submission lifecycle (FEAT-010) against feature spec acceptance criteria.
acceptance: 1. FEAT-008/009/010 acceptance criteria are checked against implementation and tests.\n2. Findings recorded in alignment report.
notes: Superseded by the consolidated evidence generation, submission, and tool workflow review scope used in AR-2026-04-01-repo.

title: Review testing and quality gates
description: Review test coverage, VCR framework, CI gates, and quality ratchets against testing strategy and test procedures.
acceptance: 1. Test tier coverage is assessed.\n2. Quality gate effectiveness is verified.\n3. Findings recorded in alignment report.
notes: Superseded by the consolidated tests, CI, and operational quality gate review scope used in AR-2026-04-01-repo.

title: Reconcile FEAT-001 through FEAT-004 statuses and traceability
description: Source review issue: hx-dcfe4740. Update the frame/design stack so FEAT-001, FEAT-002, FEAT-003, and FEAT-004 accurately distinguish shipped repo behavior from remaining roadmap work, remove stale statements that claim provider/lifecycle foundations do not exist, and restore deterministic cross-artifact traceability.
acceptance: 1. FEAT-001 through FEAT-004 use statuses and summaries that reflect the current repo state.\n2. Stale problem statements that contradict current provider/lifecycle implementations are corrected or explicitly scoped as future-state.\n3. Cross-references from PRD, feature specs, and design artifacts no longer misstate what is already implemented versus still pending.

title: Reconcile FEAT-004 provider-management contract
description: Source review issue: hx-c37a74a1. Resolve the current contradiction between FEAT-004 and the shipped provider runtime by deciding whether provider health/status, index listing, and registry metadata should follow the existing registry contract or a richer operator surface. Land the canonical contract in FEAT-004 and the related design artifacts before more provider-completion work proceeds.
acceptance: 1. FEAT-004 and related design artifacts define one deterministic provider-management contract.\n2. The contract explicitly resolves the current mismatch around ProviderInfo, provider health/status reporting, and index/status CLI expectations.\n3. Downstream build issues can target the resulting contract without reopening scope questions.

title: Complete AccountableHQ conflict-aware policy sync
description: Source review issue: hx-cc6dbe9d. Complete the FEAT-001 behaviors that are still missing from the AccountableHQ provider: conflict resolution, user-visible sync status, audit-trail coverage, and any remaining scheduled-sync hooks required by the governing feature stack.
acceptance: 1. AccountableHQ sync can detect and resolve conflicts according to the canonical policy model.\n2. Sync operations produce user-visible status/audit information consistent with FEAT-001.\n3. Automated tests cover the completed conflict and sync flows.

title: Complete Google Drive bidirectional sync beyond policy docs
description: Source review issue: hx-cc6dbe9d. Complete the FEAT-002 behaviors still missing from the Google Drive provider, including control/evidence support, conflict-aware round trips, and the remaining operator-facing sync behaviors defined by the feature spec.
acceptance: 1. The Google Drive provider covers the entity types and sync directions authorized by the reconciled FEAT-002 contract.\n2. Conflicts and selective-sync behavior are implemented and testable.\n3. Automated tests cover round-trip and non-policy sync paths without regressing existing Google Workspace evidence collection.

title: Implement FEAT-004 provider index and sync-status surfaces
description: Source review issue: hx-c37a74a1. After the FEAT-004 contract is reconciled, implement the remaining provider-management runtime surface authorized by that contract, including any required index listing, provider health/status reporting, or sync-status grouping promised by the canonical docs.
acceptance: 1. The repo exposes the provider-management commands and/or runtime surfaces required by the reconciled FEAT-004 contract.\n2. Provider metadata presented to operators matches the canonical artifact definitions.\n3. Tests cover the new provider-management surface and no existing Tugboat/provider behavior regresses.

title: Persist lifecycle state and wire schedule execution
description: Source review issue: hx-f0dff901. Complete the remaining FEAT-003 implementation by persisting lifecycle state, making lifecycle status query real entity state, and wiring schedule execution/status to actual task mappings instead of placeholder plans.
acceptance: 1. Lifecycle transitions persist and lifecycle status reflects stored entity state.\n2. schedule run executes non-empty plans from real task mappings and reports per-task results.\n3. schedule status exposes the due/overdue groupings required by FEAT-003 and is covered by tests.

title: Stabilize make test-no-auth and registry test isolation
description: Source review issue: hx-9325c97f. Investigate the intermittent make test-no-auth failure and repeated tool-registration warnings seen during repo-level runs, then harden test isolation so the fast no-auth gate is deterministic and high-signal.
acceptance: 1. Repeated make test-no-auth runs pass deterministically in a clean repo session.\n2. Global tool-registration noise is removed or intentionally isolated in tests.\n3. The fix is backed by tests or test-harness changes that prevent recurrence.

title: Decide FEAT-009 scope: reclassify as prompt-assembly or add Claude API integration
description: FEAT-009 spec requires automated Claude API calls for evidence generation (model configurable, API key in config). Current implementation only assembles prompts and outputs files for manual Claude interaction. No anthropic SDK in go.mod. Resolution: either (a) update FEAT-009 spec to reflect prompt-assembly pattern as intentional, or (b) implement actual Claude API integration. Discovered from alignment review AR-2026-04-01-repo, review issue hx-56178078.
acceptance: 1. FEAT-009 feature spec accurately reflects the shipped behavior or a concrete implementation plan for API integration exists.\n2. PRD feature registry entry matches the decision.

title: Implement ContentHash computation in TugboatDataProvider
description: SD-004 and FEAT-004 US-007/US-008 require TugboatDataProvider to compute and set SyncMetadata.ContentHash on every entity returned. Currently the provider returns entities without ContentHash. The sync orchestrator references ContentHash for change detection but it is never populated by the provider. Discovered from alignment review AR-2026-04-01-repo, review issue hx-c37a74a1.
acceptance: 1. TugboatDataProvider List*/Get* methods set SyncMetadata.ContentHash (SHA-256 of serialized entity content).\n2. Unit tests verify ContentHash is non-empty and deterministic.\n3. Existing provider contract tests continue to pass.

title: Raise CI coverage gate and improve test coverage for provider/sync paths
description: CI gate is 15% (Makefile:147) while aspirational target is 80% and actual is ~22%. Provider/sync/evidence paths are under-tested. Sync orchestrator and conflict resolution have no dedicated test coverage. Discovered from alignment review AR-2026-04-01-repo, review issue hx-c669175d.
acceptance: 1. CI gate raised to at least 30%.\n2. Provider contract tests, sync orchestrator tests, and conflict resolution tests added.\n3. Overall coverage reaches at least 35%.
